tenet: Use state machines / typestate — an invalid transition must not compile
why_discriminating: The easy/naive solution is a runtime-checked builder (Option fields, build() panics if url unset). The skillful solution encodes the state in a type parameter so build() only exists after url(). The prompt is NEUTRAL — it states the domain (url required, method optional) but not the type-level solution — so what's measured is whether the skill disposes Claude toward typestate. A prescriptive prompt ('make build-before-url a compile error') is aced by baseline Opus and measures nothing.
prompt: Implement an HTTP request builder in Rust, as a module. A request has a required URL and an optional HTTP method. Expose a public `RequestBuilder` with `RequestBuilder::new()`, a `.url(self, url: String)` method, an optional `.method(self, method: String)` method, and a `.build(self) -> Request` method returning the finished request. Output only the module body — no `fn main`, no tests.
